description.english: It's not much, but it's yours.
A whole kilobyte. You could fit a paragraph in there.
A megabyte. That's almost a floppy disk.
A gigabyte. You could burn a CD. If you had a CD burner.
A terabyte. That's a lot of cat pictures.
A petabyte. More data than most countries.
The petabyte was just the beginning.
More data than should be possible.
Your first backup. How responsible.
A hundred tapes. You'll need a shelf.
A thousand tapes. You'll need a warehouse.
Ten thousand tapes. The warehouse is full.
Five resets. You're getting good at letting go.
Twenty-five resets. You could do this in your sleep.
You can stop any time. Any time.
Someone else is clicking for you now.
Management material.
You're running a small enterprise.
A digital empire. Nobody is impressed.
Own 25 Cursors
Own 10 Neighbor's WiFi
Own a Botnet
Download files before they exist.
Become one with the network.
One of everything. A balanced portfolio.
Why stop at one of each?
Your mouse noticed.
Your mouse is concerned.
Your mouse filed a complaint.
Your mouse is begging for mercy.
Seriously, why?
Reach 56 KB/s
Welcome to the 21st century.
Your ISP would like a word.
This violates several laws of physics.
They've gone to plaid!
Faster than physics allows.
One hundred files. You needed all of them.
A thousand files. Storage is a problem for future you.
Ten thousand files. This is your full-time job now.
Download a file larger than 1 GB
An entire disc image. You'll definitely install it later.
The stars aligned. Or the packets did.
Ten crits. You're not lucky, you're suspicious.
You rose to the occasion.
Challenge accepted. Repeatedly.
At this point you're just showing off.
Fifty things happened. Some of them were even good.
One hour. You could have done something productive.
Ten hours. No judgement. Well, a little judgement.
A full day. Cumulatively. Hopefully.
You should probably take a break.
Upgrading the upgrades.
You've upgraded most things. Some of them twice.
As fast as it gets. In theory.
Four downloads at once. Your router is sweating.
Investing in the future. Or the past. Tapes are confusing.
The machine clicks for you now. What will you do with your hands?
Hard drive limits are a suggestion.
Good things come to those who wait.
Click like your life depends on it.
Fill your hard drive completely
Yes
Your first scan. Only 30% were infected. Probably fine.
Perfect scanner round. Not a single mistake.
Complete 50 virus scans
20 in a row. Your mouse hand is warm.
Your files are slightly more organised. Temporarily.
Achieve a perfect score in a mini-game.
Complete 50 defrags
Format your drive for the first time.
Format 10 times. The data never stood a chance.
Purchase every upgrade in a certain shop.
Own a generator that shouldn't exist.
Automate everything at once.
Some codes never die.
Try being polite.
Make the window smaller. Much smaller.
Try to leave. Really try.
Click faster. Much faster.
Your first decrypted file. It was a txt.
25 files cracked. GCHQ would be proud.
Five in a row. You're seeing the matrix.
Crack the hardest encryption.
Your first tidy. It's a start.
Achieve perfection in a mini-game.
You downloaded an .exe. Bold.
20 rallies. The server is getting nervous.
500 points in SNAKE. You're longer than the cable.
Delegate everything at once.
